data.body:
PASS 1 review (A1 — anti-slop/over-engineering/NIH): This sweep is exemplary on the A1 axis. It introduces no new abstraction, dependency, or config surface — it reuses the codebase's already-canonical `logging.getLogger(__name__)` convention (documented in `src/rebar/_logging.py`, used at 30+ existing call-sites) and the existing `# noqa: BLE001 — <reason>` pattern (95+ existing sites). It explicitly NET-REMOVES config surface (allowlist entries at pyproject.toml:213-215). `_logging.py:33` even pre-empts the NIH trap by mandating "no shared swallow helper", and the plan respects that. No golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization / premature-abstraction concerns. One grounded scope-ambiguity finding below; otherwise clean for A1.

- [ ] Reconcile AC3+AC4 with the existing allowlist intent that `_engine_support` T201 prints are *legitimate, not transitional*. pyproject.toml:195-201 explicitly classes the `_engine_support` data-CLIs (reads_cli/validate/next_batch) as "legitimate-print surfaces … [that] stay print-bearing", and pyproject.toml:211-212 scopes the d360 sweep to BLE001 only ("_engine_support T201 is the data-CLI stdout contract"). But AC3 says "Stderr diagnostics migrate to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`" and AC4 says "These packages removed from the allowlist" — taken together that reads as removing the `T201` token from `"src/rebar/_engine_support/*" = ["BLE001", "T201"]`, which would force ~30 legitimate `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` usage/error lines (e.g. reads_cli.py:48/56/57, validate.py:270-317, next_batch.py:446+) onto the logger. State whether T201 stays on `_engine_support/*` (BLE001-only removal, matching the comment) or whether the stderr-diagnostic migration is in-scope; if the latter, confirm the data-contract stdout `print(json.dumps(...))` sites are preserved. (location: AC line 3 & 4 vs pyproject.toml:195-215; criteria: A1 — config-surface)
